visual_attributes.morphology: elongated body with a long tail, covered in large, overlapping scales
distinctive_features: brown keratin scales covering the body
long, sticky tongue for collecting ants and termites
granular skin on feet with pads on front feet
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: head-body length 40–65 cm, tail length 35–56 cm, weight up to 10 kg
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: scales arranged in rows covering the body, fibrous hair underneath

functional_attributes.primary_function: insectivore, primarily feeding on ants and termites
secondary_functions: rolling into a ball for defense

domain_specific_functional.REPRODUCTION: annual birth of one or two offspring

relationships.description: Both are in the subgenus Paramanis
Tigers are one of the natural predators of the Sunda pangolin
Native to regions including Myanmar, Thailand, and Malaysia

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Myanmar, Thailand, Cambodia, Laos, Malaysia, Singapore, Vietnam, Borneo, Java, Sumatra, Lesser Sunda Islands
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Used in traditional armor by indigenous peoples
